seoTitle: Insert and Manage Anchor Links in HubSpot
metaDescription: Create jump links in HubSpot pages and blogs by setting anchor IDs and linking text to them, including cross-page anchor links and mobile checks.
intro: Anchor links let visitors jump straight to a specific section of a page instead of scrolling through everything, which is especially handy on long landing pages or blog posts. This guide covers setting a unique anchor ID on a header, linking text or a button to it, and even linking to an anchor on a completely different page. It wraps up with checking that jump links behave correctly on mobile.

steps.heading: Open your page or blog editor
steps.body: Go to the target website page, blog post, or landing page you want to add anchor links to.

steps.heading: Insert an anchor tag
steps.body: Place your cursor near the header section you want as a destination and click the Insert Anchor option in the rich-text toolbar.

steps.heading: Name your anchor
steps.body: Type a unique, hyphenated ID for the anchor so it can be referenced later.

steps.heading: Link text to the anchor
steps.body: Highlight your navigation text or button and click the link icon to create the hyperlink.

steps.heading: Format the anchor URL
steps.body: Type a pound symbol followed by your anchor ID to link within the same page, or append it to a full URL for cross-page jumps.

steps.heading: Test and publish
steps.body: Review the jump behavior in the mobile preview mode before publishing the page live.

faq.question: What is an anchor link used for?
faq.answer: It lets visitors click a link and jump directly to a specific section of a page instead of scrolling manually.

faq.question: Can I link to an anchor on a different page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can append the anchor ID to a full page URL to jump to that section from anywhere on your site.

faq.question: Do anchor links work well on mobile?
faq.answer: They generally do, but it's worth testing in the responsive preview since layout shifts can affect jump positioning.

faq.question: Do I need to edit the page's code to add an anchor?
faq.answer: No, HubSpot's rich-text toolbar includes a built-in Insert Anchor option, so no code editing is required.
